options.label: A
options.text: Two forces acting in the same direction on an object

options.label: B
options.text: Two forces that are equal in size and opposite in direction

options.label: C
options.text: A single force pushing an object forward

options.label: D
options.text: Forces that only act on stationary objects

correct_answer: Two forces that are equal in size and opposite in direction
explanation: Balanced forces are two forces of equal magnitude acting in opposite directions on an object. They cancel each other out, resulting in no change in the object's motion. If the object is at rest, it stays at rest; if it's moving, it continues at the same speed and direction.
